---
title: "Services & FAIR Data"
subtitle: "Tools and services provided by Huma-Num"
toc: true
---

## FAIR Principles in Practice

The **FAIR principles** — Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable — are central to Huma-Num's mission and to open science policy in France and Europe.

::: {.callout-note}
Since 2021, the **Plan national pour la science ouverte** requires that research data from publicly-funded projects be deposited in open, FAIR-compliant repositories. Horizon Europe grants impose similar requirements at the European level.
:::

🔗 [go-fair.org/fair-principles](https://www.go-fair.org/fair-principles/) · [DMP OPIDoR](https://dmp.opidor.fr)

---

## Core Services

### NAKALA — Research Data Repository

**NAKALA** is Huma-Num's main data publishing platform. It allows researchers to deposit and share humanities research data openly, with:

- **Persistent ARK identifiers** for each deposited item
- Rich metadata description (Dublin Core, custom schemas)
- Open access licences (Creative Commons)
- FAIR-compliant infrastructure, connected to EOSC

🔗 <https://nakala.fr>

---

### Isidore — Discovery Platform

**Isidore** is a search and discovery engine aggregating open access scholarly publications, data, and resources in the humanities and social sciences. It harvests metadata from hundreds of French and international sources.

Key features: semantic enrichment (thematic, geographical, temporal), multilingual search, integration with HAL, OpenEdition, NAKALA, and DARIAH services.

🔗 <https://isidore.science>

---

### Stylo — Semantic Text Editor

**Stylo** is a web-based semantic writing tool designed for academic writing in the humanities. It uses Markdown, YAML, and BibTeX to separate content from formatting, enabling rich export to HTML, PDF, XML-TEI, DOCX, and EPUB.

Originally developed by CRIHN (Université de Montréal) and maintained in partnership with Huma-Num.

🔗 <https://stylo.huma-num.fr>

---

### Huma-Num Box

A cloud storage solution (based on Nextcloud) for collaborative work on research projects. Provides file sharing, versioning, and basic collaborative editing for research teams.

🔗 <https://box.huma-num.fr>

---

### Virtual Machines (HN-SSH)

On-demand **virtual computing environments** for projects that require custom software stacks, databases, or web services. Particularly useful for hosting digital editions, project databases, or web applications. Access via a project submission process.

🔗 <https://www.huma-num.fr/les-services-et-les-outils/>

---

### SSH Open Marketplace

Co-maintained with DARIAH and CLARIN, the **SSH Open Marketplace** is a discovery portal for tools, services, training materials, workflows, and datasets for the social sciences and humanities. Integrated into EOSC.

🔗 <https://marketplace.sshopencloud.eu>

---

## Data Management Plans

For doctoral researchers, a **Data Management Plan (DMP)** is increasingly required by funding bodies (ANR, ERC, Horizon Europe). Useful resources:

- **DMP OPIDoR** — the French national DMP tool (Inist-CNRS): [dmp.opidor.fr](https://dmp.opidor.fr)
- Consortium-specific guidance for disciplinary data (available via each consortium)
- UPPA research data team: [recherche.univ-pau.fr/donnees-de-la-recherche](https://recherche.univ-pau.fr/fr/science-ouverte/donnees-de-la-recherche.html)

---

## Access for UPPA Researchers

Huma-Num services are primarily accessible to researchers affiliated with CNRS, universities, and research institutions in France. UPPA doctoral students can access most services through their institutional credentials.

For onboarding and questions, contact the UPPA research data service or your doctoral school administrator.
